commands.author: cathrynlavery

target.title: fix(agents/harness): validate forced plugin harness support before pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `selectAgentHarnessDecision()` returned the forced plugin harness for a candidate without consulting that harness's own `supports({ provider, modelId })` predicate. Once an agent's runtime was pinned to a plugin harness (e.g. `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`), every fallback-chain candidate flowed through that harness regardless of whether the harness could actually serve the candidate's provider.
- **Why it matters:** For Codex, this surfaced in production as a late `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` whenever an operator's fallback chain referenced an OpenClaw-only provider that was absent from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Observed on operator installs that have a local proxy provider (`9router`) configured under OpenClaw `models.providers` but not registered in Codex's own `[model_providers.*]` config. Combined with the long startup hang fixed in #74335, this turned a single cross-config gap into recurring overnight outages where every agent's tool calls dropped to zero.
- **What changed:** In the forced-plugin branch of `selectAgentHarnessDecision()`, call `forced.supports({ provider, modelId, requestedRuntime })` before pinning. If unsupported, route to PI when fallback policy permits, otherwise throw an unsupported-harness error including the candidate `provider/modelId` and the harness's `reason`. Extend Codex harness `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` from `{"codex"}` to `{"codex", "openai", "openai-codex"}` to match the documented Codex routing ids (`docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`) and the existing `resolveCodexAppServerModelProvider()` mappings in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** The auto-mode harness selection (`runtime === "auto"`) was already calling `supports()` correctly. Session pinning precedence (`agentHarnessId` over env / config) is preserved. The behavior of `runWithModelFallback()` candidate enumeration is unchanged. Codex app-server protocol is untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #74335 (this repo, the same operator outage's symptom-amplification fix bounding `withCodexStartupTimeout`)
- Related #42715 (Multi-provider AI Gateway: missing apiKey causes silent model fallback) — adjacent class of cross-provider fallback misroute
- Related #48680 (Model fallback treats HTTP 403 business rejection as `candidate_succeeded`) — adjacent class of fallback-decision correctness
- Related #64902 (Failed primary model attempt not logged when fallback succeeds — no forensic audit trail) — orthogonal observability gap
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` forced-plugin branch returned the harness whose `id === runtime` without consulting its `supports()` predicate. The harness's own opinion about which providers it can serve was bypassed entirely whenever the runtime was pinned, so cross-config fallbacks reached Codex's app-server, which then rejected them with `failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` after sending the request through `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts:97-101`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** `harness.supports()` exists on every `AgentHarness` and is consulted in the auto-mode branch (`src/agents/harness/selection.ts:134-141`), but the forced branch trusted the runtime id alone. There was no contract test forcing every harness selection path through `supports()`.
- **Contributing context:** Codex's `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` set was historically `{"codex"}` only, even though the documented native path is `openai/gpt-*`. The forced-branch bypass made the narrow set invisible. Tightening the selector (this PR) requires the set to match documented behavior, hence the `openai`/`openai-codex` additions.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:** Unit test asserting the forced-plugin selection branch consults `supports()` for the candidate `provider/modelId`, plus a contract test for the Codex harness's accepted provider ids.
- **New tests added:**
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: (1) forced plugin harness with unsupported candidate provider falls back to PI when permitted, (2) raises clear error when fallback is `none`, (3) session-pinned plugin harness rejects unsupported candidate provider, (4) compaction routing for a plugin-pinned session whose provider the plugin does not support fails closed.
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: 2 pre-existing pin-precedence tests updated to use a Codex-supported provider so they continue to test their original intent.
  - `extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` (new file): Codex harness `supports()` accepts `codex`, `openai`, `openai-codex` (case-insensitive) and rejects foreign provider ids such as `9router`.

## Architecture / Flow Diagram

```text
Before:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ return { harness: codex, ... }              ← supports() never consulted
        └─ Codex app-server: "Model provider 9router not found"   (late RPC error)

After:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ forced.supports({ provider: "9router", modelId, requestedRuntime: "codex" })
        ├─ supported: true   → return forced harness          (when codex actually owns it)
        └─ supported: false
              ├─ policy.fallback === "pi"   → return PI harness, log warn
              └─ policy.fallback === "none" → throw "Requested agent harness 'codex' does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: ...) and PI fallback is disabled."
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: npm-global install of `openclaw@2026.4.26`
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary; `9router` configured as an OpenClaw provider but not in `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway-internal failure
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`; agents pinned to `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`; `models.providers["9router"]` defined under OpenClaw

### Steps

1. Configure an OpenClaw agent with `agentRuntime.id: "codex"` and a fallback chain that synthesizes a `9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6` candidate (via `models.providers["9router"]`).
2. Provoke a primary-model failure (e.g. transient codex startup hang per #73520; or simply force codex offline for a turn).
3. Observe `gateway.err.log`: fallback chain advances `openai-codex/gpt-5.5 → openai/gpt-5.5 → 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6`; the last candidate fails with `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider 9router not found` and `next=none`.

### Expected

- The fallback chain skips a candidate the forced harness cannot serve, either routing it to the embedded PI backend (when `policy.fallback === "pi"`) or surfacing a clear unsupported-harness error (when `policy.fallback === "none"`).

### Actual (before this PR)

- Codex receives the unsupported provider and produces the `Model provider X not found` error after the request has already been sent into the harness, with `next=none` ending the chain.

## Evidence

- Failing trace before (extracted from operator's `gateway.err.log`):
  ```text
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"
  ```
- Passing tests after:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` → 26/26 pass.
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` → 4/4 pass.
- Verified locally:
  - `pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on all 5 modified files.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "openai"` / `modelId: "gpt-5.4"` continues to be pinned (documented canonical path; covered by new codex harness test and unaffected pin-precedence test).
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "pi"` routes to PI with a warn log; `selectedReason: "forced_plugin_fallback_to_pi"`.
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "none"` throws `Requested agent harness "codex" does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: codex, openai, openai-codex) and PI fallback is disabled.`
  - Session-pinned (`agentHarnessId: "codex"`) with unsupported provider throws (covered by new test).
  - Compaction (`maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession`) routes the unsupported pinned-session candidate to the same throw path (covered by new test).
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Auto-mode selection (`agentRuntime.id: "auto"`) is unchanged; existing tests pass.
  - PI runtime forced (`agentRuntime.id: "pi"`) unchanged.
  - CLI runtime aliases unchanged.
  - `agentHarnessId` (session pin) precedence over `agentRuntime` defaults preserved.
- **What I did NOT verify:**
  - Did not run `pnpm check` (full prod sweep) or `pnpm test` (full suite) locally; relied on targeted test files plus core+extensions typecheck and the changed-files gate. CI should run the broader gates.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` for any deployment where the forced plugin harness already supported the candidate provider via its `supports()` predicate (the documented Codex paths `openai/*`, `openai-codex/*`, `codex/*` continue to pin Codex). Behavior changes only for cross-provider candidates the harness could not have served correctly anyway: those now either route to PI (when permitted) or surface a clear unsupported-harness error instead of producing a late `Model provider X not found` from the underlying harness.
- Config/env changes? `No`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Some installs may have relied on the forced-pin bypass to route `azure-openai/*` (or another non-canonical id) through Codex's app-server.
  - **Mitigation:** Codex harness can be extended easily — add the additional id to `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` or accept an explicit `providerIds` override on `createCodexAppServerAgentHarness({ providerIds: [...] })`. The current set covers the documented canonical paths; additional ids should be added with explicit owner-extension review.
- **Risk:** The new error message on `policy.fallback === "none"` is surfaced through synchronous selection rather than via a model-fallback decision row.
  - **Mitigation:** The error message is structured (includes provider/modelId/harness id and reason), so log/observation code can match on it. The previous failure mode produced a late `CodexAppServerRpcError` from a different layer, which was harder to attribute to the harness selection decision.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Forced plugin harness selection now consults the harness `supports()` predicate before pinning, so unsupported fallback providers do not get sent into Codex app-server and fail late as "Model provider X not found".
- **Real environment tested:** macOS operator OpenClaw gateway setup, npm-global OpenClaw install, agent pinned to Codex runtime with an OpenClaw-only `9router` fallback provider absent from Codex config. Evidence is from the operator gateway/runtime path described above, with secrets redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the PR branch harness-selection runtime checks and inspected the real gateway failure path/log excerpt that motivated the patch. Commands used: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the operator setup and PR branch checks:

  ```text
  gateway.err.log before patch (real operator setup):
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"

  after patch, targeted runtime checks:
  pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts -> 26/26 pass
  pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts -> 4/4 pass
  pnpm tsgo:core -> clean
  pnpm tsgo:extensions -> clean
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched branch routes unsupported forced-Codex candidates to PI when fallback is permitted, or throws a clear unsupported-harness error when fallback is disabled, instead of invoking Codex app-server with a provider it cannot load.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check` / full repository test sweep was not run locally; broader CI should still run before landing.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

## Summary

Fixes #68864.

- discover valid `{session}.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl` transcript files when `sessions.json` is missing `compactionCheckpoints`
- include disk-discovered checkpoints in `sessions.list` row count/latest preview and `sessions.compaction.list/get`
- allow branch/restore to operate on disk-discovered checkpoints, not just metadata-backed checkpoints

## Tests

- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-shims:$PATH pnpm check:changed -- --base origin/main`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '<inline script creating a real SessionManager transcript, checkpoint snapshot, then calling listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync>'`
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts -t "discovers checkpoint transcript files" --reporter=verbose` (stopped after the local Vitest/Rolldown build stayed in plugin timing output for over two minutes without reaching test assertions)
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts -t "discovers valid checkpoint" --reporter=verbose` (same local Vitest/Rolldown build stall)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Web UI/session APIs no longer report an empty checkpoint list when checkpoint transcript files exist on disk but `sessions.json` has no `compactionCheckpoints` metadata.

Real environment tested: Local Linux workspace on this branch with the OpenClaw monorepo at `origin/main` base `3a2220f0a5`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'created a real SessionManager session transcript, captured a .checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl snapshot, omitted compactionCheckpoints metadata, called listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync, and required one disk checkpoint with matching leaf/sessionFile'
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the direct runtime command:

```text
{"discovered":1,"checkpointId":"0f65407f-a898-498e-bcb4-112096da3818","hasLeaf":true,"hasSessionFile":true}
```

Observed result after fix: The real transcript/checkpoint setup returned exactly one disk-discovered checkpoint and included both the pre-compaction leaf id and snapshot file path.

What was not tested: Local Vitest execution did not complete on this host because the test runner stayed in the Rolldown plugin build phase; this PR relies on GitHub CI for actual Vitest execution.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 new tests: deferred settlement for Telegram, eager settlement for non-caption channels, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn on Telegram.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `6de206309d`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test file (`dispatch-acp.test.ts` with 54 tests) to unpatched `upstream/main` (`a002c416c7`) source. 1 test fails:

```
 ❯ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests | 1 failed) 47ms

 FAIL  auto-reply > tryDispatchAcpReply > does not call settleVisibleText before TTS synthesis when mode is final
 AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality

 - Expected
 + Received

 - false
 + true

 ❯ dispatch-acp.test.ts:1928:35

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 53 passed (54)
```

The failing test proves that without this patch, `settleVisibleText()` is called **before** TTS synthesis (`settleCalledBeforeTts === true`), confirming the churn bug.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/tts-final-text-before-audio-83511
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 54 tests pass including the 4 new tests covering: deferred settlement for Telegram (caption-capable channel), eager settlement for non-caption channels (Discord), text fallback when TTS synthesis fails, and immediate settlement when TTS mode is not "final".
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 54 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, non-final, and channel-gating paths.

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(heartbeat): suppress fallback after route-matched message tool
target.body:
When heartbeat runs in message-tool-only mode, a successful generic message tool delivery can still be followed by duplicate free-text fallback narration in the same Telegram chat.

Fixes #84217

## Affected surface

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`
  - Adds route-matched message tool delivery evidence for heartbeat replies only after the generic message tool sent to the same provider/target/account route.
- `src/auto-reply/reply-payload.ts`
  - Adds internal reply-payload metadata for route-scoped message tool delivery evidence.
- `src/auto-reply/heartbeat-reply-payload.ts`
  - Adds a heartbeat-specific evidence reader.
- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`
  - Suppresses free-text fallback only when the reply carries route-scoped message tool delivery evidence.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.ts`
  - Leaves heartbeat payloads available for heartbeat-specific route evidence instead of dropping them via generic message-tool dedupe first.
- Tests:
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`

## Scope

This is a parameter/evidence threading fix. It does not add a config flag, does not change heartbeat scheduling, and does not touch Slack identity, commitment, or channel-specific private paths.

The important semantic boundary is that "generic message tool delivery evidence" is not the same as "has outbound reply content". This PR only marks heartbeat replies when existing message-tool target evidence matches the same delivery route via the existing `resolveMessagingToolPayloadDedupe` route matcher. Global `messagingToolSentTexts` without route targets is intentionally not enough.

After Vincent's thread-route follow-up, I found one extra current-head issue while refreshing proof: generic reply-payload dedupe could drop the matched heartbeat payload before `heartbeat-runner` saw the route evidence. The current head keeps heartbeat payloads out of that generic dedupe layer so the heartbeat-specific suppression path receives the evidence and can still preserve no-route / wrong-topic fallback behavior.

## Prior art

Prior attempt #84273 was closed by contributor due to inability to provide live channel proof. This PR provides expanded source + runtime helper proof, with the helper semantic explicitly distinguishing "generic message tool delivery evidence" from "has outbound reply content" to address the message-delivery risk flagged in #84273.

The key difference from #84273 is route scope: #84273 marked heartbeat payloads after any message-tool send, while this PR only marks a heartbeat payload when committed `messagingToolSentTargets` match the reply route. If the message tool delivered to another route, or only global text evidence exists, heartbeat fallback still sends.

## Current-head P1 follow-up proof

This update addresses the ClawSweeper P1 finding "Suppress same-route fallback even when the text differs" on head `25958135ed2135dede29e716668ac34cfaea8007`.

The same-route heartbeat evidence rule now treats committed route-matched generic `message` tool target evidence as sufficient for a single plain heartbeat fallback payload, even when the fallback text differs from the tool body. Multi-payload heartbeat replies keep the existing text-match guard so unrelated fallback payloads are not suppressed. Rich payloads, media payloads, reasoning, no-route evidence, wrong-route evidence, and wrong-thread/topic evidence remain outside this suppression path.

Commands run locally:

```text
$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts -t "same-route message-tool delivery used different text"
RED before implementation:
AssertionError: expected undefined to be true

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts -t "same-route message-tool delivery used different text"
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 46 skipped (47)

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  47 passed (47)

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts -t "heartbeat replies"
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed | 55 skipped (64)

$ pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
exit 0

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
exit 0

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
INF no leaks found
```

### Updated live Telegram proof for head `25958135ed2135dede29e716668ac34cfaea8007`

The earlier Mantis Telegram workflow could not run because this actor lacks write/maintain/admin permission, so I reran the current-head proof locally with the existing Telegram proof credential and a temporary harness outside the patch. The harness used the real current-head route-evidence path:

- `buildReplyPayloads(...)` built heartbeat payloads from route-scoped `messagingToolSentTargets`.
- `runHeartbeatOnce(...)` made the heartbeat delivery/suppression decision.
- Telegram delivery used the real Bot API `sendMessage` path.
- Route, chat, topic, token, local paths, and timestamps are redacted.

Visible Telegram screenshot: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84975#issuecomment-4524650870

```json
{
  "proof": "openclaw-pr-84975-heartbeat-live-proof",
  "head": "25958135ed2135dede29e716668ac34cfaea8007",
  "mode": "live-telegram",
  "route": {
    "provider": "telegram",
    "to": "telegram:group:<redacted-chat>",
    "thread": "topic:<redacted-thread>"
  },
  "scenarios": [
    {
      "scenario": "same-route-different-text",
      "expected": "suppress",
      "result": "PASS",
      "decision": "suppress",
      "evidence": {
        "mode": "same-route",
        "targetCount": 1,
        "metadataDelivered": true,
        "targetRoute": "telegram:group:<redacted-chat>"
      },
      "transport": {
        "messageToolSendCount": 1,
        "heartbeatFallbackSendCount": 0,
        "sends": [
          { "phase": "message-tool", "method": "sendMessage", "status": "ok", "message_id": "15" }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "scenario": "same-route-same-text",
      "expected": "suppress",
      "result": "PASS",
      "decision": "suppress",
      "evidence": {
        "mode": "same-route",
        "targetCount": 1,
        "metadataDelivered": true,
        "targetRoute": "telegram:group:<redacted-chat>"
      },
      "transport": {
        "messageToolSendCount": 1,
        "heartbeatFallbackSendCount": 0,
        "sends": [
          { "phase": "message-tool", "method": "sendMessage", "status": "ok", "message_id": "16" }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "scenario": "no-route",
      "expected": "send",
      "result": "PASS",
      "decision": "send",
      "evidence": {
        "mode": "none",
        "targetCount": 0,
        "metadataDelivered": false,
        "targetRoute": "none"
      },
      "transport": {
        "messageToolSendCount": 0,
        "heartbeatFallbackSendCount": 1,
        "sends": [
          { "phase": "heartbeat-fallback", "method": "sendMessage", "status": "ok", "message_id": "17" }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "scenario": "wrong-thread",
      "expected": "send",
      "result": "PASS",
      "decision": "send",
      "evidence": {
        "mode": "wrong-thread",
        "targetCount": 1,
        "metadataDelivered": false,
        "targetRoute": "telegram:group:<redacted-chat> topic:<wrong-thread-redacted>"
      },
      "transport": {
        "messageToolSendCount": 0,
        "heartbeatFallbackSendCount": 1,
        "sends": [
          { "phase": "heartbeat-fallback", "method": "sendMessage", "status": "ok", "message_id": "18" }
        ]
      }
    }
  ],
  "summary": { "pass": 4, "fail": 0 }
}
```

Observed visible Telegram messages match the decisions above: same-route different-text only shows the message-tool visible body, same-route same-text only shows one visible body, and the no-route / wrong-thread cases show heartbeat fallback messages.



## Current-head maintenance proof

After Vincent's route/thread follow-up moved this PR to `f21343bfd4`, CI exposed one current-head regression: `checks-node-auto-reply-reply-agent-runner` timed out in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` at `dedupes duplicate same-target reply text without suppressing unrelated finals`.

Root cause: route dedupe called `getChannelPlugin("telegram")` from the generic reply-payload path, which lazily loads the bundled Telegram plugin in this test/runtime path and can block the shard. Current head `df6f33925b` keeps the route/thread semantics from Vincent's follow-up, uses only the already-loaded channel registry for plugin-specific matching, and preserves internal route-string fallback for forms like `telegram:group:<chat>:topic:<thread>` without loading bundled channel implementations.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** heartbeat message-tool-only delivery should not send duplicate free-text fallback after the generic `message` tool has already delivered to the same route, but it must still send fallback when no same-route delivery evidence exists.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0, branch `wolf/heartbeat-suppress-after-message-tool`, head `14b8854108`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran a local OpenClaw runtime helper that imports production `runHeartbeatOnce`, seeds a real `openclaw.json`-shaped config and session store, registers a local Telegram outbound adapter, then executes three heartbeat ticks: same-route evidence, no-route evidence, and wrong-thread/topic-unmarked evidence.
- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the runtime helper:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=14b8854108 pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr84975-proof/runtime-proof.mts

```

- **Observed result after fix:** with route-scoped message-tool evidence, `runHeartbeatOnce` completes and does not invoke the Telegram send path (`telegramSendCount: 0`), so the fallback narration is suppressed. Without route evidence, and for the wrong-topic/wrong-thread-unmarked fallback case, the same runtime path invokes the Telegram send path once (`telegramSendCount: 1`), proving fallback remains available when the message tool did not prove same-route delivery.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram heartbeat tick with a real bot token/chat. I did not test Slack/WhatsApp live channels; the patch is heartbeat reply evidence and generic runner fallback suppression, with Telegram-shaped runtime coverage for the reported duplicate free-text path.

target.title: feat: add scoped mention pattern policy
target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(discord): keep forced voice consult diagnostics private
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop forced Discord realtime consult fallbacks from appending internal consult-policy diagnostics to the agent-facing voice prompt.
- Apply the same private-diagnostic treatment to phone-call realtime forced consults while preserving the forced-consult reason in server debug logs.
- Add Discord voice regression coverage and update voice-call expectations; add an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "forces an agent consult from final user transcript when consult policy is always"`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/voice/realtime.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Realtime voice forced consult fallback no longer feeds internal consult-policy diagnostics into model-facing prompts that can be spoken back to users.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with mocked Discord realtime voice and voice-call realtime provider harnesses.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the Vitest commands listed above after moving the patch onto a clean branch from origin/main; after the review-comment fix, reran the Discord e2e file, the focused voice-call forced-consult regression, oxfmt check, and `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: Discord regression asserts the agent command message is exactly `What?` and does not contain `consultPolicy` or `openclaw_agent_consult`; voice-call tests assert forced consult args include only the caller question; both forced fallback paths now log the private forced-consult reason through server debug logging instead of passing it to the model/tool payload.
Observed result after fix: Discord voice e2e file passed; voice-call forced-consult regression passed; diff whitespace check passed; touched files are oxfmt-clean; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on the original patch.
What was not tested: A live Discord or phone call with real OpenAI realtime audio was not run.

target.title: fix(update): avoid broad tag fetches for dev updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw update --channel dev` could fail before updating `main` when a release tag on the remote conflicted with an existing local tag.
- Solution: make dev updates fetch branches/remotes with `--no-tags`, while keeping release-channel tag fetching unchanged.
- What changed: explicit dev tag targets now fetch only the requested tag from configured remotes before resolving it.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stable/beta updates remain tag-driven and still fetch tags broadly.

## Motivation

- This fixes the observed beta-to-dev switch failure where an unrelated moved beta tag blocked a dev update that should track git `main`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw update --channel dev` no longer performs a broad tag fetch that can fail on unrelated moved release tags before updating from `main`.
- Real environment tested: Local bare git remote plus clean checkout reproducer, using real `git fetch` and `git rebase` commands with a moved conflicting `v2026.5.19-beta.2` tag.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a local bare remote, cloned it, moved the remote beta tag, confirmed current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` fails, then confirmed PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags` followed by `git rebase origin/main` succeeds.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
proof_root=<temp-redacted>

old dev broad tag fetch (current main behavior):
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
   c39fe2e..63b8b7c  main              -> origin/main
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)

reset checkout to before proof fetch:
clean_status=0

new dev fetch + rebase (PR behavior):
local_tag_before=c39fe2e
remote_tag_after=63b8b7c
local_tag_after_no_tags_fetch=c39fe2e
head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes

release-channel broad tag fetch remains tag-sensitive:
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)
```

Supplemental focused test proof:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 46 passed (46)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real git reproducer shows the old broad tag fetch fails with the same `would clobber existing tag` class of error, while the PR-style dev fetch avoids tag mutation and the checkout rebases to `origin/main`. A broad tag fetch still fails afterward, preserving the proof that release-channel tag-driven behavior remains tag-sensitive.
- What was not tested: A live openclaw-prod update was not run from this agent.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): User-reported failure showed `openclaw update --channel dev` rejected `v2026.5.19-beta.2` during broad tag fetch with `would clobber existing tag`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dev updates used the same broad tag-fetch behavior as release-channel updates even though dev is branch/main based.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests covered dev update fetch failures but did not assert that dev avoids broad tag fetching.
- Contributing context (if known): Switching from beta to dev can leave local release tags around, and Git rejects clobbering changed tags unless forced.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dev updates fetch with `--no-tags`; explicit dev tag targets fetch only the requested tag; failed targeted tag fetches do not fall back to stale local tags.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The update runner already injects the git command runner, so these tests verify the exact command contract without needing a mutable remote fixture.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing dev update tests covered fetch sequencing but not the tag-fetch mode.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw update --channel dev` now ignores unrelated remote tags during the normal branch update path. Explicit dev tag targets still fetch the requested tag.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --tags -> unrelated tag conflict -> update fails

After:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --no-tags -> rebase main -> update continues
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The git fetch command changes for dev updates only. It narrows normal dev fetch behavior by suppressing tag fetches; explicit tag targets fetch only the requested tag ref.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux worktree
- Runtime/container: Node via repo test wrapper; local git bare remote proof
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): update CLI git mode
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Create a local bare git remote and clone it.
2. Create local tag `v2026.5.19-beta.2`, then move the remote tag to a newer commit.
3. Run current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` and observe tag-clobber failure.
4. Reset the checkout to clean state, run PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags`, then `git rebase origin/main`.
5. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Current-main-style broad tag fetch fails on the moved tag.
- PR-style dev fetch does not mutate/fetch the moved tag and branch rebase succeeds.
- Stable/beta paths continue to use tag fetching.
- Explicit dev tag targets do not use a stale local tag after targeted tag fetch failure.

### Actual

- Real git proof: broad tag fetch failed with `would clobber existing tag`; PR-style dev fetch plus rebase succeeded with `head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes`.
- `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts` passed 46/46.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real local bare-remote moved-tag reproduction, targeted update-runner tests, `git diff --check`, and `codex review --base origin/main` after rebase.
- Edge cases checked: default dev update fetch mode, explicit dev tag target, failed targeted tag fetch with a stale local tag candidate, release-channel broad tag sensitivity.
- What you did **not** verify: live update on openclaw-prod.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Explicit dev tag targets could otherwise resolve stale local tags after a failed targeted fetch.
  - Mitigation: The targeted tag fetch is forced for the requested ref and local tag resolution is skipped when all configured remote fetches fail.

target.body:
## Summary
- route the visible WebChat attachment button to the file input inside its own composer instead of using a document-wide query
- mark the toolbar control as type=button so it cannot accidentally submit surrounding forms
- add coverage for the visible button-to-hidden-input activation path

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live Chrome/Windows native file-picker smoke; this environment is headless/Linux.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: WebChat's visible attachment button must trigger the hidden file input in the same `.agent-chat__input` composer, not the first `.agent-chat__file-input` in the document. This covers issue #47983 in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux 6.19.14+kali-amd64 checkout of PR head `b0f962a4b1`; Node v22.22.2; pnpm 11.1.0 via corepack. DOM smoke used Lit + jsdom with the actual `renderChat(...)` implementation from `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Created an untracked DOM smoke script under `.artifacts/proof-83952/` that imports `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`, renders two WebChat composers, stubs both hidden file inputs' `.click()`, then clicks the second visible attachment button.
  2. `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  3. `node --import tsx .artifacts/proof-83952/webchat-attachment-button-dom-smoke.mjs`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal stdout from the DOM smoke command:
  ```json
  {
    "composerCount": 2,
    "firstAttachButtonType": "button",
    "secondAttachButtonType": "button",
    "firstFileInputInFirstComposer": true,
    "secondFileInputInSecondComposer": true,
    "clickedInputSequence": ["second-file-input"],
    "secondButtonClickedOnlyItsOwnFileInput": true
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Clicking the visible attachment button in the second rendered WebChat composer invoked only that second composer's hidden `.agent-chat__file-input`; the first composer's input was not clicked. The visible attachment buttons rendered as `type="button"`, so the click cannot submit surrounding form chrome.
- What was not tested: Did not interact with the OS-native file picker dialog, because headless DOM/browser automation cannot inspect that dialog directly. Did not run a full gateway/WebChat server or verify the later selected-file upload/send pipeline.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156625-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Preserve reporter credit for #48360 by RacecarGuy, #62762 by justinschille, and #73535 by DJBlackhawk in the PR body.; Mention #51357 by tianyaqpzm and #42019 by 0xrsydn as earlier closed reports that supplied useful historical evidence.; Do not claim or implement #64400's unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation; say that security-shaped sub-scope is routed separately.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451
